response.justification: The candidate exceeds every technical requirement for this role — deep TypeScript, React, and Node.js product experience, strong REST and architecture fundamentals, plus all the nice-to-haves including LLM/MCP work, AWS, and Kubernetes. However, this is an entry-level position and the candidate is a Berlin-based staff/senior engineer with roughly 15+ years of experience, currently employed in a staff role. The mismatch raises real questions about salary expectations, engagement, and retention in a junior IC position, and there is no explicit PostgreSQL or Nest.js exposure. Worth a conversation only if the candidate has a clear, credible motivation for stepping into this scope.
strengths: Strong full-stack TypeScript/React/Node.js experience across multiple product companies
Covers all nice-to-haves: LLM/MCP integration work, AWS (CDK, ECS-adjacent), and Kubernetes deployments
Solid engineering craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and already located in Berlin for on-site work
concerns: Significantly overqualified for an entry-level role, creating salary, engagement, and retention risk
No explicit PostgreSQL or Nest.js experience; database background is mainly MongoDB and MySQL
Only basic German, which may be a friction point in a German energy-consulting and renovation domain
